finding: Blind review A: every rewritten site classified — each `.__state.x()`→`.x()` targets a genuine action, each `.__state.field`→`.$.field` is a state read; no field left read bare in a handler, no called action turned into a non-invoked `.$` read. Proxy `$`-branch ordering + function-detection intact. Clean.

`$` and the removed `__state` both return useStore.getState(), so render-context reads that were already snapshots (AppLayout useRef-init, WorkflowWorkspaceRunCard) gain no new staleness; handler sites re-access `Stores.Chat.$.error` fresh post-await rather than a captured alias. No reactivity regression.

Actions returned resolved from getState() (hook-free) in both createStoreProxy + createLocalProxy; `__state` now falls through to the reactive/hook path (TEST-4 asserts it throws outside render). No new Rules-of-Hooks violation introduced by any conversion.

HMR-destroy now uses `oldStoreProxy.$.__destroy__()`; `$` short-circuits before the trap's store-init side-effect (TEST-9), so teardown no longer risks re-initializing a store mid-destroy. No shared-state race.
Swept action calls preserve their error handling (e.g. uploadFiles(...).catch(...)); no try/catch or promise-rejection path altered by the rename.
No added work: action access is the same single getState() lookup as before; `$` is one getState() call. No new subscriptions or renders.

Blind review B: removing `__state` from ExtractZustandState/StoreProxy is safe — desktop resolves @/core/stores to the shared ui file and its 8 `declare module` augmentations only add store entries, never reference `__state`. No live consumer references `__state`. The getState-only ExtractZustandState arm gains correct `$` typing it previously lacked.

Blind review B: real behavioral tests — load the ACTUAL createStoreProxy + real zustand + real react; only the two boundaries the factory imports-but-never-calls are stubbed (neither substitutes the unit under test). TEST-3/4 assert the render-only-throws contract via the genuine hook path; TEST-9 proves `$` short-circuits init. Loader sound.

Blind review B: grit ban mirrors no-raw-interactive-elements.grit and is registered in BOTH biome.json with correct relative paths; `$obj.__state` matches the `.member` AST only (no false-positive on `.__setState`/`.__refCount`, no match on strings/comments/bracket-access). Fires under lint:guardrails.

Blind review B (CONFIRMED): ~13 stale comments still describe the removed `__state` alias after the code was swept to `$`/direct-action (assistant/file/mcp/hub/skill sites). Documentation drift only, no runtime impact.
No security surface: purely a frontend proxy/lint/test refactor. Test stubs are debug-only test-support files (not shipped); no secrets, no auth, no network. No injection surface in the grit pattern.
No authorization logic touched — swept sites only change how a store value/action is accessed, never what permission gates a read/mutation. Server-side authz unaffected (no backend change).
No DOM/ARIA/markup change — conversions are call-site renames inside handlers/reads; rendered output identical. No accessible-name or focus change.
Blind review B: the only new string is the developer-facing lint diagnostic (not user-facing) — accurate and actionable. No user-visible copy changed.

Post-merge sweep of F2/F3/model-picker's 15 new .__state sites: file-viewer find/zoom/wordwrap + FilePreviewDrawer.closePreview + ChatHistory.setSort are actions -> direct calls; ConversationPage.conversation + text-extension Auth.user/Chat.conversation/pendingBranchFromMessageId are state reads -> $. Verified: 0 __state remain (grep), tsc + npm run check green both workspaces, ban-lint 0 violations, 81/81 unit tests pass.
